name: Pitkern-Norfolk
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Pitkern is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Pitkern-Norfolk (Pitkern on Pitcairn, Norfuk on Norfolk Island) is an English-lexifier creole with a Tahitian substrate, written in the Latin script. Spelling is NOT standardised — several competing conventions exist (e.g. the Norfuk orthographies of Buffett and of Nobbs Palmer) — so no single sourced letter→IPA table can honestly be given.
